ingredients.address: Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan

ingredients.name: Shizuoka
ingredients.address: Shizuoka, Japan

ingredients.name: Motosuko Observation Park
ingredients.address: 2926 Nakanokura, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Rin camps solo at Lake Motosuko in episode 9, gazing at the iconic Fuji-framed view from this hilltop park that is printed on the 1,000-yen bill. The wide open panorama of Mt. Fuji over the lake is one of the most celebrated images in the entire Yuru Camp series.

ingredients.name: Zo Island (Dembei Island)
ingredients.address: Zo Island (Dembei Island), Nishina, Nishiizu, Kamo District, Shizuoka 410-3514, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The girls ride out to this tiny rocky island off the Nishiizu coast in the season finale, marveling at the rugged sea scenery and the strange elephant-shaped silhouette of the rocks, capping their Izu Peninsula road trip.

ingredients.name: Food Store Aoki, Nishiizu
ingredients.localName: フードストアあおき西伊豆店
ingredients.address: Food Store Aoki, Nishiizu, 298-2 Nishina, Nishiizu, Kamo District, Shizuoka 410-3514, Japan

ingredients.name: Fujikawa Station
ingredients.address: Fujikawa Station, Nakanogō, Fuji, Shizuoka 421-3306, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nadeshiko takes the train from this station at the start of her episode 7 solo adventure into the Minamialps, and the modest riverside station sets the tone for the rural journey that follows.

ingredients.name: Naradanosato Hot Spring
ingredients.localName: 奈良田温泉 女帝の湯
ingredients.address: Naradanosato Hot Spring, 486-486 Narada, 早川町 Hayakawa, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2701, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nadeshiko's Minamialps episode 7 route takes her to this remote hot spring deep in the Hayakawa valley, soaking after a long day of exploring the Narada area while the steam rises over the surrounding mountains.

ingredients.name: Kominka Cafe Kagiya UNESCO Eco Park Station

ingredients.address: Kominka Cafe Kagiya UNESCO Eco Park Station, 1064-43 Narada, Hayakawa, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2701, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 7, Nadeshiko stops at this charming old-house cafe in the Narada district, warming up with local fare while exploring the UNESCO Minami Alps Eco Park region on her solo adventure.

ingredients.name: Unexplored Adventure Guest House Yamato Fortress
ingredients.localName: 秘境冒険民宿 山人砦
ingredients.address: Unexplored Adventure Guest House Yamōdo Fortress, 403 Narada, Hayakawa, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2701, Japan

ingredients.entityContext: Nadeshiko encounters this enormous ancient cedar, reportedly over 1,000 years old, during her Hayakawa Valley detour in episode 7, pausing in awe at its massive trunk beside the mountain road.

ingredients.entityContext: The gang wanders through this lantern-lit alley beside the Sengen Taisha gate in episode 7, where the aroma of Fujinomiya yakisoba draws Nadeshiko and friends in for a taste of the local specialty.

ingredients.name: Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha Shrine
ingredients.address: Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha Shrine, 1-1 Miyacho, Fujinomiya, Shizuoka 418-0067, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nadeshiko and the outdoor club stop at this grand shrine during their Fujinomiya episode 7 walk, enjoying the sacred atmosphere near the foot of Mt. Fuji and sampling the famous Fujinomiya yakisoba nearby.

ingredients.entityContext: The camping club stays at Misaki Campsite on the shore of Lake Yamanakako in episode 5, waking up to a crisp morning view of Mt. Fuji reflected in the lake just steps from their tents.

ingredients.entityContext: After camping at Lake Yamanakako in episode 5, the girls head to this onsen to soak with a famous view of red-tinged Mt. Fuji at dusk, which gives the facility its name Benifuji (Crimson Fuji).

ingredients.entityContext: The outdoor club visits Oshino Hakkai spring ponds in episode 5, where crystal-clear snowmelt from Mt. Fuji wells up through the ground, and Nadeshiko is captivated by the impossibly clear blue water.

ingredients.name: Minobu Station
ingredients.address: Minobu Station, Tsunouchi, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2412, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Rin boards the Minobu Line at this small station near the start of Season 2 episode 4, using it as a transit point on her solo journey toward new camping spots along the Minobu region.

ingredients.entityContext: The outdoor club stops at this hilltop observation park during episode 3 to look out over the vast inland sea of Lake Hamana, spotting the narrow Imagire channel where the lake meets the ocean.

ingredients.entityContext: The outdoor club eats unagi (eel) at this beloved restaurant on the shores of Lake Hamana in episode 3, celebrating the meal as a quintessential regional treat on their road trip.

ingredients.address: Hamanako-Sakume Station, Mikkabichō Sakume, Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu, Shizuoka 431-1401, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nadeshiko and Chiaki spot a flock of white storks roosting on the torii gate at this tiny lakeside station during the Hamana Lake episode 3 trip, a quirky real-world spectacle that becomes one of the trip highlights.
